Here is a concise picture of **everything under `components/`** as it exists in your repo right now.

## What the components are for

Together they implement a **single marketing landing page** for **Asaastance**: enterprise / AI consulting positioning, with SEO, automation, and app-development services. The home page wires them in this order: `Navbar` → `HeroSection` → `TrustedBySection` → `ServicesSection` → `SuccessStoriesSection` → `ExpertsSection` → `CTASection` → `Footer` (see `app/page.tsx`).

## Section components (8 files)

**`Navbar`** (`"use client"`): Sticky top bar with logo (stacked-layers SVG + wordmark), desktop links to `/`, `/services`, `/case-studies`, `/success-stories`, `/experts` (labeled “Company”), and “Book a Consultation” → `/inquiry`. Mobile: full-screen overlay menu with the same links and CTA; `useState` toggles open/close; Lucide `Menu` / `X`.

**`HeroSection`**: Two-column hero—badge “The future of enterprise”, headline about transforming enterprise with intelligent solutions, supporting copy about AI research → business impact, two buttons (“Book a Consultation”, “View Our Work”—the latter is outline only, no `Link`). Right side: `next/image` `/hero-server.png` with a glassmorphism testimonial overlay (CTO quote, 40% efficiency).

**`TrustedBySection`**: Narrow band with “Trusted by industry leaders” and five **placeholder** client logos (initial letter in a square + name; grayscale row).

**`ServicesSection`**: `id="services"`, intro “Comprehensive Digital Solutions”, responsive grid of **eight** service cards. Each card uses your `Card` primitives, a Lucide icon, title, description, two bullet “features”, and wraps the whole card in `Link` to `/services/{slug}` (slugs like `blog-automation`, `technical-seo`, etc.).

**`SuccessStoriesSection`**: `id="case-studies"` but the UI title is “Client Success Stories”. Dark (`#141824`) two-column grid of story tiles with images (`/fintech-graph.png`, `/robot-arm.png`), industry tag, title, description, and two stat blocks each. “View All Case Studies” links to `#case-studies` (same section), not a listing page.

**`ExpertsSection`**: `id="experts"`, centered heading “Meet the Experts”, four team members with photos (`/expert-*.png`), name, role, and a LinkedIn icon linking to `#` (placeholder).

**`CTASection`**: Full-width **primary-colored** band: headline about “next industrial revolution”, workshop copy, two buttons (“Book Your Consultation” secondary styling, “Contact Sales” ghost)—**neither is wrapped in `Link`**; they are plain `Button`s.

**`Footer`**: Dark footer (`#11131c`): brand block (same logo SVG + tagline + social icons to `#`), **Services** column with real `Link`s matching service slugs, **Company** and legal links mostly `#`, **Offices** list (SF, London, Singapore), copyright © 2024.

## UI primitives (`components/ui/`)

**`button.tsx`**: Shadcn-style `Button` built with `class-variance-authority` for variants (`default`, `destructive`, `outline`, `secondary`, `ghost`, `link`) and sizes; uses `Slot` from `radix-ui` for `asChild`; merges classes with `cn` from `@/lib/utils`.

**`card.tsx`**: Composable card pieces (`Card`, `CardHeader`, `CardTitle`, `CardDescription`, `CardAction`, `CardContent`, `CardFooter`) with `data-slot` attributes and Tailwind layout helpers—used heavily by `ServicesSection`.

## Cross-cutting patterns

- **Stack**: React + Next.js (`Link`, `Image`), Tailwind, Lucide icons, CVA + `cn` for styling.
- **Theming**: Mix of semantic tokens (`primary`, `muted-foreground`, `border`) and hard-coded colors (e.g. success stories background, footer).
- **Content vs routing**: Several CTAs and social/expert links are still **placeholders** (`#` or buttons without navigation). Navbar routes assume pages like `/case-studies` and `/success-stories` exist elsewhere.

That is the full picture of what your `components` tree contains and how it behaves narratively and structurally. If you want the same level of detail for `app/` routes or `lib/`, say which area to open next.

## PAGE 1: HOME

### Hero Section
**Headline:** ASAASTANCE

**Subheadline:** AI, SaaS, Tech Consultancy

**Tagline:** Bespoke AI and SaaS solutions, simplified, to solve complex problems.

**Body:** We learn the complex, scary technology so you don't have to. Then we customize it specifically for your business. Nothing more. Nothing less.

**CTA Buttons:** [Get Started] [Learn More]

---

### What We Do Section

**AI Solutions**
Intelligent automation for repetitive tasks. Billing, collections, monitoring, reporting - all automated.

**SaaS Customization**
Software tailored to your workflow. Not one-size-fits-all. Built for how you actually work.

**Tech Consultancy**
Strategy, implementation, and ongoing support. We guide you through the AI revolution.

---

### How It Works Section

**1. Discovery**
We audit your workflows and identify automation opportunities.

**2. Design**
We build ASaaSTance Agents specifically for your needs.

**3. Deployment**
We integrate, train your team, and optimize continuously.

---

## PAGE 2: WHAT WE OFFER (All Agents)

### Core Agents

**BILLING AUTOMATION AGENT**

What it does:
- Generates invoices automatically based on policy data
- Tracks payment status in real-time
- Sends payment reminders at 7, 14, and 30 days
- Flags overdue accounts for human follow-up
- Updates accounting software automatically

Benefit: Dramatic reduction in manual billing work

---

**COLLECTION AUTOMATION AGENT**

What it does:
- Monitors all outstanding payments daily
- Sends personalized reminder emails/SMS
- Offers payment plan options to clients
- Documents all collection attempts
- Escalates to human agent when needed

Benefit: Improved collection efficiency, less awkward conversations

---

**POLICY MONITORING AGENT**

What it does:
- Tracks all policy expiration dates
- Alerts 30, 15, and 7 days before renewal
- Identifies coverage gaps in client portfolios
- Flags life changes that may need policy updates
- Prepares renewal quotes automatically

Benefit: Never miss a renewal, proactive client service

---

**CLAIMS ASSISTANCE AGENT**

What it does:
- Organizes claim documentation
- Tracks claim status with carriers
- Updates clients on progress automatically
- Flags urgent claims requiring immediate attention
- Maintains claims history database for compliance

Benefit: Faster claim resolution, happier clients

---

**ACCOUNTING & REPORTING AGENT**

What it does:
- Reconciles daily transactions across all accounts
- Generates weekly and monthly financial reports
- Tracks commission payments to agents
- Monitors cash flow and alerts to anomalies
- Prepares data for tax filing and audits

Benefit: Clean books, no spreadsheet chaos

---

**CONTENT MARKETING AGENT**

What it does:
- Writes blog posts about industry topics and trends
- Creates email newsletters for client education
- Develops educational content for prospects
- Generates SEO-optimized website copy
- Repurposes content across multiple platforms

Benefit: Consistent content without writer's block

---

**SOCIAL MEDIA AGENT**

What it does:
- Creates and schedules posts for Facebook, LinkedIn, Instagram
- Monitors comments and direct messages
- Tracks engagement metrics and growth
- Identifies trending topics in your industry
- Responds to common questions automatically

Benefit: Active social presence, reduced manual posting time

---

**GRAPHIC DESIGN AGENT**

What it does:
- Accesses library of pre-designed Canva templates
- Customizes templates with your brand colors, logos, text
- Auto-generates social media graphics from content briefs
- Resizes designs for multiple platforms
- Works with Content Marketing and Social Media Agents

How it works:
1. Content Marketing Agent writes post text
2. Graphic Design Agent selects appropriate template
3. Auto-fills: headline, body text, brand colors, logo
4. Presents 2-3 variations for approval
5. Upon approval, delivers final files

Template Library Includes:
- Quote graphics (insurance tips, advice)
- Announcement graphics (new policies, updates)
- Educational infographics (coverage types, claims process)
- Holiday greetings (seasonal posts)
- Testimonial layouts (client reviews)

Limitations:
- Works from templates, not original designs
- Complex custom work requires human designer
- Brand must be established first (colors, fonts, logo)

Benefit: Consistent, on-brand visuals without design software skills

## PAGE 3: US VS. COMPETITORS

| **Them** | **Us** |

## PAGE 4: SIX STEPS TO INTEGRATION

**Step 1: Discovery Call (Day 1)**
- Deep dive into your current workflows
- Identify biggest pain points and time wasters
- Map your existing tech stack
- Define success metrics

**Step 2: Custom Design (Days 2-5)**
- Design ASaaSTance Agents for your specific needs
- Configure integrations with your systems
- Create customized dashboards
- Build workflow automations

**Step 3: Setup & Integration (Days 6-10)**
- Deploy agents to your environment
- Connect to your existing software
- Import client and business data
- Test all automations thoroughly

**Step 4: Training (Days 11-12)**
- Train your team on the new system
- Show them how to monitor agents
- Practice handoff procedures
- Review exception handling

**Step 5: Soft Launch (Days 13-20)**
- Run parallel with existing processes
- Monitor agent performance
- Fine-tune workflows based on feedback
- Gather input from your team

**Step 6: Full Deployment & Optimization (Ongoing)**
- Switch to full agent operation
- Weekly check-ins for first month
- Monthly optimization reviews
- Quarterly strategy sessions

**Timeline:** 3 weeks to full deployment
**Support:** Continuous from day one

**Our Story**

ASaaSTance was founded by two entrepreneurs who saw the AI revolution coming years ago. We've spent years preparing to help businesses navigate this transformation.

**Co-Founder: D.E. Wilson**

Serial entrepreneur with 20+ years experience in copywriting, marketing, sales, management, and technology. Former General Manager managing 100+ person teams across Asia. Founded startups in Singapore, Indonesia, and the Philippines.

**Co-Founder: John Ranel Intong**

12+ years in Web Development, Data, Education, and Graphic Design. Former IT Director managing 20+ people and part of executive team overseeing 100+ staff. Recognized as one of Cebu's most talented Web Developers.

**Our Philosophy**

Most AI solutions are one-size-fits-all. They force your business into their mold. We do the opposite. We learn the complex, scary technology so you don't have to. Then we customize it specifically for your business. Nothing more. Nothing less.

Here is a structured read of the **new content document** and how it relates to **what your site already is**, so you can plan replacements and additions without ambiguity.

## What this document is

It is a **full copy deck** for roughly **seven logical pages** (Home plus six topical pages), not just hero text. It repositions the brand around **bespoke AI + SaaS + consultancy**, **ASaaSTance Agents** (many are **insurance-adjacent**: billing, collections, policy, claims), and **concrete pricing** (PHP + USD). Developer notes add **brand colors**, **Montserrat-style sans**, **responsive layout**, **contact form**, and **prominent pricing**.

## Page-by-page understanding

**PAGE 1 — HOME**  
- **Hero:** Brand name as headline, short positioning (AI / SaaS / consultancy), tagline + body that match your existing “we learn the tech for you” idea but in sharper, shorter form.  
- **CTAs:** “Get Started” and “Learn More” (replacing “Book a Consultation” / “View Our Work” style CTAs in the current hero).  
- **What We Do:** Three pillars — AI Solutions, SaaS Customization, Tech Consultancy — each with a tight paragraph. This maps conceptually to a **new section** (or a rewrite of **Trusted By + Services** territory), not a 1:1 match to your current eight service cards.  
- **How It Works:** Three steps — Discovery → Design (ASaaSTance Agents) → Deployment. This is **new narrative structure**; your live site does not currently have an equivalent block.

**PAGE 2 — WHAT WE OFFER**  
- A **catalog of “Core Agents”**: eight agents with “What it does” bullets, optional “How it works” / template library / limitations (Graphic Design), and a **Benefit** line each.  
- This is **much more productized and vertical** than your current **ServicesSection** (blog automation, technical SEO, generic dev). Replacing or supplementing services here is a **strategic choice**: either swap the grid for agents, or run both (agents as primary, old services as secondary).

**PAGE 3 — US VS. COMPETITORS**  
- A **comparison table** (Them vs Us) plus a closing “bottom line.”  
- **New page or section**; nothing like it exists in the current component set.

**PAGE 4 — SIX STEPS TO INTEGRATION**  
- **Six timed steps** (Day 1 through ongoing), bullets per step, **3-week timeline** and **continuous support** callout.  
- **New page or long section**; complements “How It Works” on home but is **more operational detail**.

**PAGE 5 — PRICING**  
- **Four packages** (A–D) with PHP/month, USD equivalents, feature bullets, “best for” lines.  
- **Package D** adds hardware (custom PC specs) and an **early adopter** price, with API costs called out.  
- Your current site has **no pricing UI**; this is a **major add**. Notes ask for a **clear, prominent pricing table**.

**PAGE 6 — ABOUT**  
- **Origin story**, two **named co-founders** with bios, **philosophy** (echoes home body copy).  
- Maps to a real **/about** (or similar) page; your **ExpertsSection** is currently **fictional personas** — this doc **replaces that narrative** with the actual founders unless you keep both intentionally.

**PAGE 7 — CONTACT**  
- **Get in Touch** headline, **placeholder emails/phones** for each founder, **office hours**, **next steps** (pilot vs assessment).  
- Notes: **contact form** required. Your **Navbar** already points to **/inquiry** for consultation; contact content may live there or on **/contact**.

## Branding and naming consistency

- Document uses **ASaaSTance** / **ASaaSTance Agents**; your UI currently uses **Asaastance** in the logo wordmark. You will want **one canonical spelling/casing** for nav, metadata, and headings.  
- **Agent** is your product metaphor; the old site leans **enterprise AI consulting** and **generic digital services**. The new copy is **more specific** (workflows, insurance-style agents, Canva templates, etc.).

Adding **&#96;/what-we-offer/[slug]&#96;** later is straightforward if you want per-agent SEO without blocking the first release.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  Home[\"/\"]\n  Offer[\"/what-we-offer\"]\n  Vs[\"/vs-competitors\"]\n  Steps[\"/integration\"]\n  Price[\"/pricing\"]\n  About[\"/about\"]\n  Inquiry[\"/inquiry\"]\n  Home --\u003e Offer\n  Home --\u003e Steps\n  Home --\u003e Price\n  Home --\u003e About\n  Home --\u003e Inquiry\n  Offer --\u003e Inquiry\n  Price --\u003e Inquiry\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## 1. Single source of truth for copy (non-negotiable for “robust”)\n\nAdd a typed content module so pages stay layout-focused and future edits do not require hunting through JSX.\n\n- **New file:** e.g. [&#96;lib/site-content.ts&#96;](lib/site-content.ts) (or &#96;content/site.ts&#96;) exporting:\n  - &#96;homeHero&#96;, &#96;homeWhatWeDo&#96; (3 items), &#96;homeHowItWorks&#96; (3 steps)\n  - &#96;agents[]&#96; — each with &#96;id&#96;, &#96;title&#96;, &#96;whatItDoes: string[]&#96;, optional &#96;howItWorks&#96;, &#96;limitations&#96;, &#96;templateLibrary&#96;, &#96;benefit&#96;\n  - &#96;comparison&#96; — rows for the Us vs Them table + bottom line\n  - &#96;integrationSteps&#96; — six steps with day ranges and bullets\n  - &#96;pricingPackages&#96; — A–D with PHP/USD, features, “best for”, and Package D PC spec lines\n  - &#96;about&#96; — story, founders (name, bio), philosophy\n  - &#96;contact&#96; — headline, office hours, next-step options; **founder emails/phones as constants** (or &#96;process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_*&#96;) so placeholders are not left in JSX\n\n**Rule:** Page components import from this module only; no duplicate prose between Home and inner pages.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Brand system: colors, font, metadata\n\n**Colors (your spec):** light blue &#96;#5DADE2&#96;, white &#96;#FFFFFF&#96;, dark text &#96;#2C3E50&#96;.\n\n- Update CSS variables in [&#96;app/globals.css&#96;](app/globals.css) &#96;:root&#96; so **&#96;--primary&#96;** maps to the brand blue (convert hex → OKLCH to match existing token style), **&#96;--foreground&#96;** / body text toward &#96;#2C3E50&#96;, keep **&#96;--background&#96;** white for light mode.\n- **Dark mode:** Your brief is light-first. Either (a) tune &#96;.dark&#96; tokens to remain harmonious with the new primary, or (b) temporarily **de-emphasize dark** (navbar already supports &#96;dark:&#96; classes)—choose one consistent approach so contrast still passes WCAG for primary-on-white and white-on-primary buttons.\n\n**Font:** Replace &#96;Inter&#96; with **Montserrat** in [&#96;app/layout.tsx&#96;](app/layout.tsx) via &#96;next/font/google&#96;, wire &#96;variable&#96; into &#96;body&#96;, and align &#96;@theme inline&#96; in &#96;globals.css&#96; (&#96;--font-sans&#96;) with that variable so Tailwind &#96;font-sans&#96; is not lying (today &#96;layout&#96; uses &#96;--font-inter&#96; while &#96;@theme&#96; references Geist—worth fixing as part of the same change).\n\n**Metadata:** Update &#96;title&#96; / &#96;description&#96; in &#96;layout.tsx&#96; (and add **per-route** &#96;metadata&#96; or &#96;generateMetadata&#96; on new pages) to match the new positioning (AI + SaaS + agents), not “enterprise LLM integration” alone.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Shared page shell (DRY, fewer bugs)\n\nMost routes repeat &#96;\u003cNavbar /\u003e&#96; … &#96;\u003cFooter /\u003e&#96; with ad-hoc wrapper classes ([&#96;app/page.tsx&#96;](app/page.tsx), [&#96;app/services/page.tsx&#96;](app/services/page.tsx), [&#96;app/inquiry/page.tsx&#96;](app/inquiry/page.tsx)).\n\n- Add **&#96;components/SiteShell.tsx&#96;** (or &#96;app/(site)/layout.tsx&#96; route group) that wraps children with consistent &#96;min-h-screen&#96;, background, and optional main padding.\n- Migrate existing pages to the shell **when touching them** (home, inquiry, new pages first) to avoid a giant unrelated refactor.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Navigation and footer (IA + kill broken links)\n\n**Navbar** ([&#96;components/Navbar.tsx&#96;](components/Navbar.tsx)):\n\n- Replace &#96;navLinks&#96; with the new primary IA, for example: **Home**, **What We Offer** (&#96;/what-we-offer&#96;), **Integration** (&#96;/integration&#96;), **Pricing** (&#96;/pricing&#96;), **Vs Competitors** (&#96;/vs-competitors&#96;), **About** (&#96;/about&#96;), **Get Started** CTA → &#96;/inquiry&#96; (maps to your “Get Started”; “Learn More” on the hero can target &#96;/about&#96; or &#96;/what-we-offer&#96;—define explicitly in content constants).\n\n**Footer** ([&#96;components/Footer.tsx&#96;](components/Footer.tsx)):\n\n- Replace the long list of old SEO/dev service links with **Agents** (anchor links to &#96;/what-we-offer#agent-id&#96; or top of page) **or** a short link group: Offer, Pricing, Integration, About, Contact/Inquiry.\n- Update company column to real routes; remove **placeholder &#96;#&#96;** links where you now have real pages (Privacy/Terms can stay &#96;#&#96; until you have documents).\n\n**Critical fix:** [&#96;components/ServicesSection.tsx&#96;](components/ServicesSection.tsx) and the footer currently point at **&#96;/services/{slug}&#96;** routes that **do not exist** in &#96;app/&#96;. As part of migration, **remove or retarget** those links to &#96;/what-we-offer&#96; (and anchors)—do not leave 404s.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Home page composition ([&#96;app/page.tsx&#96;](app/page.tsx))\n\nAlign HOME in your doc with these sections (top to bottom):\n\n1. **Hero** — Replace [&#96;components/HeroSection.tsx&#96;](components/HeroSection.tsx) content: headline **ASAASTANCE**, subhead **AI, SaaS, Tech Consultancy**, tagline + body from doc; CTAs **Get Started** (&#96;Link&#96; to &#96;/inquiry&#96;) and **Learn More** (&#96;Link&#96; to chosen target). Keep or swap imagery intentionally (doc does not specify an image).\n2. **What We Do** — New component: three columns (AI Solutions, SaaS Customization, Tech Consultancy).\n3. **How It Works** — New component: three steps (Discovery, Design, Deployment)—shorter than the six-step integration page; include a **secondary link** to &#96;/integration&#96; for detail.\n4. **Optional:** Retain [&#96;components/TrustedBySection.tsx&#96;](components/TrustedBySection.tsx) only if you still want social proof; your new HOME text does not require it—default recommendation: **drop from home** to avoid mixed messaging, or move below CTAs.\n5. **Remove or repurpose:** [&#96;components/ServicesSection.tsx&#96;](components/ServicesSection.tsx) (old eight services) should **not** stay as-is on home; the product story is now **agents** on &#96;/what-we-offer&#96;. [&#96;components/SuccessStoriesSection.tsx&#96;](components/SuccessStoriesSection.tsx) is absent from the new doc—**remove from home** unless you are keeping it as optional storytelling (then rewrite copy to real cases later).\n6. **Team teaser:** Replace [&#96;components/ExpertsSection.tsx&#96;](components/ExpertsSection.tsx) on home with a **short “Meet the founders”** strip linking to &#96;/about&#96;, or remove entirely if About is enough.\n7. **CTA** — Update [&#96;components/CTASection.tsx&#96;](components/CTASection.tsx) copy to match pilot/workshop language from Contact/Pricing context; wire buttons to &#96;/inquiry&#96; and optionally &#96;/pricing&#96;.\n8. **Footer** — as above.\n\n---\n\n## 6. New pages (App Router)\n\nImplement as server components unless interactivity demands &#96;\"use client\"&#96;.\n\n| Route | Purpose | Implementation notes |\n|-------|---------|----------------------|\n| [&#96;app/what-we-offer/page.tsx&#96;](app/what-we-offer/page.tsx) | All Core Agents | Map &#96;agents&#96; from &#96;site-content&#96;; each agent: title, bullet list, benefit; Graphic Design agent gets subsections (how it works, templates, limitations). Use semantic headings (&#96;h2&#96;/&#96;h3&#96;), anchor &#96;id&#96;s for footer deep links. |\n| [&#96;app/vs-competitors/page.tsx&#96;](app/vs-competitors/page.tsx) | Comparison table | &#96;\u003ctable\u003e&#96; with &#96;\u003cthead\u003e&#96;, scoped mobile layout (horizontal scroll or stacked cards—pick one pattern and test). |\n| [&#96;app/integration/page.tsx&#96;](app/integration/page.tsx) | Six steps | Vertical stepper / numbered list; emphasize **3 weeks** and **continuous support** from doc. |\n| [&#96;app/pricing/page.tsx&#96;](app/pricing/page.tsx) | Packages A–D | Prominent responsive cards; Package D: nested spec list + **early adopter** pricing + explicit **API costs** disclaimer; consider a small footnote section for legal clarity (financing, exclusions). |\n| [&#96;app/about/page.tsx&#96;](app/about/page.tsx) | Story + founders + philosophy | Two-column founder bios (photo optional); reuse philosophy from doc. |\n\nEach page: &#96;metadata&#96;, &#96;SiteShell&#96;, consistent section spacing, primary CTAs to &#96;/inquiry&#96;.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Inquiry / Contact ([&#96;app/inquiry/page.tsx&#96;](app/inquiry/page.tsx))\n\n- Replace generic “Global Headquarters” / fake SF details **unless** they are still accurate—your PAGE 7 specifies **office hours**, **two named contacts**, and **next steps** (pilot vs assessment).\n- Add **radio or select** for “Next step” (pilot vs assessment) to the form if you want operational alignment with the copy (optional but matches the doc).\n- Keep &#96;handleSubmit&#96; behavior until you wire a backend (Formspree, Resend, server action + email, CRM). **Document** in code comment or README that submission is currently client-side navigation to thank-you page only.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Legacy routes cleanup (avoid user confusion)\n\n- **&#96;/services&#96;:** Replace content with a **301/redirect** to &#96;/what-we-offer&#96; **or** rewrite the page to a short “We’ve reorganized our offerings” with one CTA—avoid two competing “services” narratives.\n- **&#96;/case-studies&#96;, &#96;/success-stories&#96;, &#96;/experts&#96;:** Not in new IA; remove from nav/footer and either redirect or leave unlinked (decision in §Decisions).\n- **Thank-you page** [&#96;app/inquiry/thank-you/page.tsx&#96;](app/inquiry/thank-you/page.tsx): Update copy to ASaaSTance voice and fix any links that assume old positioning.\n\n---\n\n## 9. Quality bar before you call it done\n\n- **Link audit:** No &#96;href&#96; to non-existent routes; all navbar/footer CTAs resolve.\n- **Accessibility:** Form labels associated, table headers marked, heading order logical on long agent page.\n- **Responsive:** Pricing cards and comparison table usable on small screens.\n- **Performance:** Hero &#96;Image&#96; &#96;priority&#96; only where needed; large agent page is static content (fine).\n\n---\n\n## Suggested implementation order (minimize thrash)\n\n1. &#96;site-content.ts&#96; + types (all copy in one place).\n2. Tokens + Montserrat + root metadata.\n3. &#96;SiteShell&#96; + Navbar/Footer IA update + fix broken &#96;/services/*&#96; links in components.\n4. New routes (offer, integration, pricing, vs, about) using shared section primitives if helpful (&#96;PageHeader&#96;, &#96;ProseSection&#96;).\n5. Home refactor (Hero, What We Do, How It Works, CTA, strip old sections).\n6. Inquiry page merge with PAGE 7 + optional &#96;/contact&#96; redirect.\n7. Legacy &#96;/services&#96; and optional redirects for old marketing pages.\n8. Pass through metadata + manual QA.\n",

Here is what was added to the plan (in [asaastance_content_rollout_aeb7157c.plan.md](c:\Users\John Ranel\.cursor\plans\asaastance_content_rollout_aeb7157c.plan.md)):

### Updates made

1. **Overview** — Now explicitly includes shipping **differentiated UI/UX** so the site does not read as a generic template or default “AI” styling.

2. **Decisions** — New bullet: **visual differentiation** is in scope from the start, with a pointer to **§10**.

3. **New todo: `art-direction-uiux`** — Covers asymmetry / editorial grids, varied section patterns (not uniform card stacks), tuned shadcn primitives, light texture/motion, **sticky TOC** on `/what-we-offer`, focus states, and `prefers-reduced-motion`.

4. **`theme-font-meta` todo** — Extended to **paired typography** (Montserrat + a complementary body font), not Montserrat-only.

5. **`home-refactor` / `legacy-cleanup` todos** — Tied to **§10** layouts and **§10.4** design QA.

6. **New §10 — UI/UX and art direction** — Full subsection structure:
   - **10.1** — Patterns to avoid (centered-everything, repeated 3-card grids, purple gradients, default Card stacks, cliché stock).
   - **10.2** — Concrete principles: type pairing, asymmetric layout rhythm, brand surfaces / section templates, disciplined use of `#5DADE2` / `#2C3E50`, subtle texture, restrained motion, long-page nav for agents, responsive comparison UX, pricing hierarchy + Package D clarity, inquiry scan hierarchy.
   - **10.3** — Where it lands in code (`globals.css`, button/card tuning, thin primitives like `Section` / `Eyebrow` / `StepRail`, navbar polish).
   - **10.4** — **Acceptance criteria** so “done” means visibly not interchangeable with a generic SaaS landing page.

7. **§5 (Home)** — “What We Do” / “How It Works” must use **§10** layouts, not only equal three-column cards.

8. **§9** — Extra UX/a11y items: **focus rings**, optional **skip link**, font/layout shift awareness.

9. **Implementation order** — Inserted an **art direction pass** early (step 2), merged **design QA (§10.4)** into the final pass, and fixed a few broken `` `**/path`** `` typos in the plan file.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I need to fix globals.css - I added `--surface-muted` in :root but didn't define it in :root - I used it in @theme but the variable --surface-muted needs to be in :root

Let me read globals.css - I replaced :root but may have removed --surface-muted definition - I had `--surface-muted: oklch(0.97 0.01 248);` in the replacement after --ring

Actually looking at my replacement I added:
`--surface-muted: oklch(0.97 0.01 248);` at end of :root block - wait I need to verify. Let me read the file.

Also I duplicated @theme inline - there are now TWO @theme inline blocks. I need to merge --color-surface-muted into the first @theme block and remove duplicate @theme.

Also --font-display in @theme references itself circularly - `--font-display: var(--font-display)` is wrong.

The next/font sets on html element:
--font-display: 'Montserrat', ... 
--font-body: 'Source Sans 3', ...

So @theme should be:
--font-sans: var(--font-body);
--font-display-family: var(--font-display); 
Actually Tailwind needs a name for utility `font-display`. In @theme inline:
```
--font-display: var(--font-display);
```
is circular.

Solution: use different theme key, e.g.
```
--font-heading: var(--font-display);
```
But next/font puts value in --font-display.

So in @theme:
`--font-heading: var(--font-display), ui-sans-serif;`

Then utility class would be font-heading if tailwind maps it.

In Tailwind v4, @theme --font-* creates font-* utilities.

So:
```
--font-sans: var(--font-body), ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
--font-heading: var(--font-display), ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
```

And use `font-heading` instead of `font-display` in components. Remove the @utility font-display block or make it:
```css
@utility font-display {
  font-family: var(--font-display), ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
}
```
That's fine - the @utility doesn't need @theme. Remove circular --font-display from @theme.

Let me read current globals and fix.
</details></think>

};

Here’s what was implemented against your plan:

### Content & IA
- **[`lib/site-content.ts`](lib/site-content.ts)** — Typed copy for home, agents, comparison, integration, pricing, about, contact, `navLinks`, and `footerQuickLinks`.
- **Routes:** [`/what-we-offer`](app/what-we-offer/page.tsx), [`/integration`](app/integration/page.tsx), [`/pricing`](app/pricing/page.tsx), [`/vs-competitors`](app/vs-competitors/page.tsx), [`/about`](app/about/page.tsx), plus existing **`/inquiry`** and **`/inquiry/thank-you`** updated.

### Design system & UX
- **[`app/globals.css`](app/globals.css)** — Brand tokens (~`#5DADE2` primary, `#2C3E50` foreground), `surface-muted`, **grain** utility, **`font-display`** utility, reduced-motion guard, wired **`--font-body`** for `font-sans`.
- **[`app/layout.tsx`](app/layout.tsx)** — **Montserrat** (display) + **Source Sans 3** (body), new default metadata and title template.
- **[`components/ui/button.tsx`](components/ui/button.tsx)** — Slightly stronger focus ring, light shadow on primary, short transition.

### Shell, nav, footer
- **[`components/SiteShell.tsx`](components/SiteShell.tsx)** — Skip-to-content link, shared `<main id="main-content">`.
- **[`components/Navbar.tsx`](components/Navbar.tsx)** — IA from `site-content`, active styles via `usePathname`, **Get Started** → `/inquiry`, **ASaaSTance** wordmark.
- **[`components/Footer.tsx`](components/Footer.tsx)** — Dark band using `bg-foreground` / `text-background`, explore links + agent anchor links to `/what-we-offer#…`.

### Home
- **[`app/page.tsx`](app/page.tsx)** — `HeroSection`, `HomeWhatWeDoSection` (asymmetric copy + stacked rows), `HomeHowItWorksSection` (timeline rail), `FoundersTeaser`, `CTASection`.
- Removed unused **Services / Success stories / Experts / Trusted by** section components.

### Long-page UX
- **[`components/OfferAgentsToc.tsx`](components/OfferAgentsToc.tsx)** — Sticky jump links; **[`components/BackToTop.tsx`](components/BackToTop.tsx)** — mobile back-to-top on `/what-we-offer`.

### Inquiry & redirects
- **[`app/inquiry/page.tsx`](app/inquiry/page.tsx)** — PAGE 7–style layout: hours, next steps, both founders with mailto/tel, form + **Next step** select; comment that submit is still client-only until a backend exists.
- **[`app/inquiry/thank-you/page.tsx`](app/inquiry/thank-you/page.tsx)** — ASaaSTance voice + links to home and `/what-we-offer`.
- **[`next.config.ts`](next.config.ts)** — `/contact` → `/inquiry`; `/services` and `/services/*` → `/what-we-offer`; `/case-studies`, `/success-stories`, `/experts` → `/about`.
- **Deleted** [`app/services/page.tsx`](app/services/page.tsx) (redirect replaces it).

### Primitives
- **[`components/SiteSections.tsx`](components/SiteSections.tsx)** — `Eyebrow`, `PageIntro`, `Section`, `SectionInner`.

`npm run build` succeeds. If TypeScript ever complains about a missing `/services` page after deleting files, delete the local **`.next`** folder and rebuild (already done once here).

**Optional next steps for you:** set `NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTACT_EMAIL_WILSON`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTACT_PHONE_WILSON`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTACT_EMAIL_JOHN`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTACT_PHONE_JOHN` in `.env.local` to replace the placeholders from [`lib/site-content.ts`](lib/site-content.ts). Legacy **`/case-studies`**, **`/experts`**, **`/success-stories`** pages still exist in the repo but are **redirected** by config; you can delete those route folders later if you want a smaller tree.

Okay, this time around, I need you to understand this new contents that I might need to replace and add to my website's existing contents:

# ASaaSTance Website Content
## Complete Text for All Pages

---

## PAGE 1: HOME

### Hero Section
**Headline:** ASAASTANCE

**Subheadline:** AI, SaaS, Tech Consultancy

**Tagline:** Bespoke AI and SaaS solutions, simplified, to solve complex problems.

**Body:** We learn the complex, scary technology so you don't have to. Then we customize it specifically for your business. Nothing more. Nothing less.

**CTA Buttons:** [Get Started] [Learn More]

---

### What We Do Section

**AI Solutions**
Intelligent automation for repetitive tasks. Billing, collections, monitoring, reporting - all automated.

**SaaS Customization**
Software tailored to your workflow. Not one-size-fits-all. Built for how you actually work.

**Tech Consultancy**
Strategy, implementation, and ongoing support. We guide you through the AI revolution.

---

### How It Works Section

**1. Discovery**
We audit your workflows and identify automation opportunities.

**2. Design**
We build ASaaSTance Agents specifically for your needs.

**3. Deployment**
We integrate, train your team, and optimize continuously.

---

## PAGE 2: WHAT WE OFFER (All Agents)

### Core Agents

**BILLING AUTOMATION AGENT**

What it does:
- Generates invoices automatically based on policy data
- Tracks payment status in real-time
- Sends payment reminders at 7, 14, and 30 days
- Flags overdue accounts for human follow-up
- Updates accounting software automatically

Benefit: Dramatic reduction in manual billing work

---

**COLLECTION AUTOMATION AGENT**

What it does:
- Monitors all outstanding payments daily
- Sends personalized reminder emails/SMS
- Offers payment plan options to clients
- Documents all collection attempts
- Escalates to human agent when needed

Benefit: Improved collection efficiency, less awkward conversations

---

**POLICY MONITORING AGENT**

What it does:
- Tracks all policy expiration dates
- Alerts 30, 15, and 7 days before renewal
- Identifies coverage gaps in client portfolios
- Flags life changes that may need policy updates
- Prepares renewal quotes automatically

Benefit: Never miss a renewal, proactive client service

---

**CLAIMS ASSISTANCE AGENT**

What it does:
- Organizes claim documentation
- Tracks claim status with carriers
- Updates clients on progress automatically
- Flags urgent claims requiring immediate attention
- Maintains claims history database for compliance

Benefit: Faster claim resolution, happier clients

---

**ACCOUNTING & REPORTING AGENT**

What it does:
- Reconciles daily transactions across all accounts
- Generates weekly and monthly financial reports
- Tracks commission payments to agents
- Monitors cash flow and alerts to anomalies
- Prepares data for tax filing and audits

Benefit: Clean books, no spreadsheet chaos

---

**CONTENT MARKETING AGENT**

What it does:
- Writes blog posts about industry topics and trends
- Creates email newsletters for client education
- Develops educational content for prospects
- Generates SEO-optimized website copy
- Repurposes content across multiple platforms

Benefit: Consistent content without writer's block

---

**SOCIAL MEDIA AGENT**

What it does:
- Creates and schedules posts for Facebook, LinkedIn, Instagram
- Monitors comments and direct messages
- Tracks engagement metrics and growth
- Identifies trending topics in your industry
- Responds to common questions automatically

Benefit: Active social presence, reduced manual posting time

---

**GRAPHIC DESIGN AGENT**

What it does:
- Accesses library of pre-designed Canva templates
- Customizes templates with your brand colors, logos, text
- Auto-generates social media graphics from content briefs
- Resizes designs for multiple platforms
- Works with Content Marketing and Social Media Agents

How it works:
1. Content Marketing Agent writes post text
2. Graphic Design Agent selects appropriate template
3. Auto-fills: headline, body text, brand colors, logo
4. Presents 2-3 variations for approval
5. Upon approval, delivers final files

Template Library Includes:
- Quote graphics (insurance tips, advice)
- Announcement graphics (new policies, updates)
- Educational infographics (coverage types, claims process)
- Holiday greetings (seasonal posts)
- Testimonial layouts (client reviews)

Limitations:
- Works from templates, not original designs
- Complex custom work requires human designer
- Brand must be established first (colors, fonts, logo)

Benefit: Consistent, on-brand visuals without design software skills

---

## PAGE 3: US VS. COMPETITORS

| **Them** | **Us** |
|----------|--------|
| One-size-fits-all solutions | Bespoke, customized for your business |
| Force you into their mold | Adapt to how you actually work |
| Maximize their profits | Maximize your results |
| Generic AI tools | Agents trained on your specific needs |
| You learn the technology | We learn it for you |
| Remote-only support | In-person setup and training |
| Monthly contracts, no flexibility | Scalable packages, upgrade anytime |
| Hidden API costs | Transparent pricing, we manage APIs |
| Impersonal support | Dedicated co-founders, real relationships |

**The Bottom Line:**
They sell you software.
We deliver solutions that actually work for your business.

---

## PAGE 4: SIX STEPS TO INTEGRATION

**Step 1: Discovery Call (Day 1)**
- Deep dive into your current workflows
- Identify biggest pain points and time wasters
- Map your existing tech stack
- Define success metrics

**Step 2: Custom Design (Days 2-5)**
- Design ASaaSTance Agents for your specific needs
- Configure integrations with your systems
- Create customized dashboards
- Build workflow automations

**Step 3: Setup & Integration (Days 6-10)**
- Deploy agents to your environment
- Connect to your existing software
- Import client and business data
- Test all automations thoroughly

**Step 4: Training (Days 11-12)**
- Train your team on the new system
- Show them how to monitor agents
- Practice handoff procedures
- Review exception handling

**Step 5: Soft Launch (Days 13-20)**
- Run parallel with existing processes
- Monitor agent performance
- Fine-tune workflows based on feedback
- Gather input from your team

**Step 6: Full Deployment & Optimization (Ongoing)**
- Switch to full agent operation
- Weekly check-ins for first month
- Monthly optimization reviews
- Quarterly strategy sessions

**Timeline:** 3 weeks to full deployment

_**Agent (default)**_


I don’t have the actual pixels from those Dribbble shots (the snippets here are empty), so this is about **how to combine that kind of reference** in principle—not a literal merge of three layouts.

### What “hybrid” usually means for those references

From the **titles alone**, the mix reads as:

- **Pixpert-style “digital agency”** — Often: strong typography, case-study or portfolio rhythm, structured sections, sometimes bolder section breaks and less “AI product” cliché.
- **Spark “AI agency”** — Often: productized story (agents, automation), darker or higher-contrast moments, tech-forward cards, diagram-like or bento grids.
- **Modern AI agency light Figma template** — Often: very clean light theme, generous whitespace, soft surfaces, predictable component rhythm (easy to implement, risk of feeling generic if you don’t customize).

A **hybrid** that works is usually: **light editorial base (template #3) + one distinctive layout device from Pixpert + one “AI product” visual language from Spark**—not all three at full volume on every section.

### How to mix without a Frankenstein site

1. **Pick one “spine”**  
   Choose a single system for **spacing, radius, grid width, and section padding** (e.g. the light template). The other two only supply **accent patterns** (hero composition, one bento block, one dark band).

2. **Limit each reference to 1–2 moments**  
   Example:  
   - Hero + one “work / proof” strip → Pixpert-like asymmetry.  
   - Mid-page “capabilities” → Spark-like bento or agent cards.  
   - Everything else → calm light surfaces so the page still feels like one brand.

3. **Lock your ASaaSTance tokens**  
   Your spec is already **#5DADE2 / white / #2C3E50**. Any Dribbble palette should be **remapped** to those tokens, not imported as new purple gradients or a second primary.

4. **Typography: one pair, one role**  
   You already planned **Montserrat + a body sans**. Hybrid layouts often fail when each reference brings a different display style. Keep **one display font** and vary **layout**, not fonts.

5. **Motion: one rule**  
   Spark-style sites sometimes over-animate. Pair with the light template by using **hover/transition on cards only** and **no scroll hijacking**.

### Fit with your current codebase

Your implementation already leans **editorial + restrained** (e.g. `SiteShell`, `SiteSections`, grain, asymmetric home blocks, sticky TOC on `/what-we-offer`). A Dribbble hybrid would mean **targeted upgrades**, e.g.:

- **From Spark-like refs:** a single **bento** or **feature grid** on home or `/what-we-offer` intro—not repeating on every page.
- **From Pixpert-like refs:** a **stronger hero** (type scale, offset image, or split with a “strip” of metrics/testimonial).
- **From the light template:** keep **global cleanliness** as the default wrapper for long copy pages (integration, pricing footnotes).

### Risks

- **Three references = three art directors** unless you constrain scope.  
- **“AI agency” Dribbbles** often rely on **illustration packs** you may not have; substitute with **real photography, subtle texture, and typographic hierarchy** so it stays yours.

### Practical next step

If you want this hybrid **applied to your repo**, switch to **Agent mode** and specify: **which page gets which reference** (e.g. “Home = Pixpert hero + Spark bento; Pricing = light template only”). I can’t change files in Ask mode.

Links you mentioned (for your own review):  
- [Pixpert Digital Agency Landing Page](https://dribbble.com/shots/21664617-Pixpert-Digital-Agency-Landing-Page)  
- [Spark AI Agency](https://dribbble.com/shots/25963956-Spark-AI-Agency)  
- [Modern AI Agency Landing Page Light Theme Figma Template](https://dribbble.com/shots/26766205-Modern-AI-Agency-Landing-Page-Light-Theme-Figma-Template)

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Fix HomeWhatWeDoSection - it still references Bot and strokeWidth on Icon - fix to IconAutomation and remove strokeWidth

Rewrite HomeWhatWeDoSection with bento grid:
- grid lg:grid-cols-12 gap-4
- Left intro stays in full width row on mobile, on lg: col-span-12 then bento OR intro col-span-4 and bento col-span-8 spanning 2 rows

Layout:
```
[ Intro text col-span-5 ]
[ Card0 col-span-7 row-span-2 tall ]

Actually:
Row1: intro (5 cols) + card0 (7 cols) tall
Row2: empty under intro? + card1 (7 cols) - messy

Better bento:
- Top: full width intro strip (eyebrow, h2, p, link)
- Below: grid 12
  - Cell A: item 0 - col-span-7 row-span-1 min-h
  - Cell B: item 1 - col-span-5
  - Cell C: item 2 - col-span-5 offset OR span 12 wide bar

Classic bento:
[ 0 large - 7 cols ] [ 1 - 5 cols ]
[ 0 continues? no - single row height ]

Use:
- i=0: lg:col-span-8 min-h-[280px]
- i=1: lg:col-span-4 
- i=2: lg:col-span-12 or lg:col-span-6 lg:col-start-7

Simpler:
grid with 
card0: col-span-12 md:col-span-8
card1: col-span-12 md:col-span-4  
card2: col-span-12 md:col-span-6 md:col-start-7

And intro above full width.

Implement:

```tsx
<SectionInner>
  <div className="mb-12 max-w-2xl lg:max-w-xl">
    ...intro...
  </div>
  <div className="grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 md:grid-cols-12 md:gap-5">
    {homeWhatWeDo.map((item, i) => {
      const gridClass = i === 0 ? "md:col-span-8 md:min-h-[260px]" : i === 1 ? "md:col-span-4 md:min-h-[260px]" : "md:col-span-6 md:col-start-7";
```

Card0 and card1 same row on md: 8+4=12
Card2: col-start-7 means right half - good

Inner card layout flex column justify-between for tall cells

Hero Section full rewrite:

```tsx
<section className="relative overflow-hidden border-b border-border">
  <div className="absolute inset-y-0 left-0 w-[min(100%,52rem)] -skew-x-3 bg-surface-muted opacity-80" /> // might skew text - bad

Better: 
- Split: left padding with vertical line accent
- Right: image with clip-path: polygon

<div className="mx-auto max-w-6xl lg:px-12">
  <div className="grid lg:grid-cols-2 gap-0">
    <div className="relative z-10 px-6 py-20 lg:py-28 lg:pr-16">
      <div className="absolute left-0 top-32 bottom-32 w-1 bg-primary rounded-full hidden lg:block" />
      ...content
    </div>
    <div className="relative lg:-mr-[calc((100vw-72rem)/2+1.5rem)] min-h-[400px]">
      <Image src="/hero-visual.svg" ... className="object-cover" />
      quote overlay
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

For lg negative margin to bleed image - use simpler approach: image inside rounded-3xl with shadow, aspect ratio, no negative margin

I'll use:
- Container max-w-6xl flex flex-col lg:flex-row lg:items-stretch gap-12 py-20
- Left: pl-6 border-l-4 border-primary 
- Right: flex-1 relative

Image: width 100% height auto with unoptimized

```tsx
<Image
  src="/hero-visual.svg"
  alt="Abstract workflow and automation visual"
  width={1200}
  height={900}
  className="h-full w-full object-cover"
  priority
  unoptimized
/>
```

Container for image: aspect-[4/3] lg:aspect-auto lg:min-h-full

OfferAgentsToc - add decorative? skip

SiteSections - add BentoCell optional - or just use classes in HomeWhatWeDo

/globals - add --elevated and utility .shadow-elevated

button/card - slight updates

inner pages - read what-we-offer page, pricing, etc for class tweaks

Run build after npm install (removed lucide)
</details></think>

Here’s what was implemented against the plan:

### Brand / chrome
- **`siteBrand.name`** is now **`Asaastance`** (matches [asaastance.vercel.app](https://asaastance.vercel.app/)).
- **[`app/layout.tsx`](app/layout.tsx)** title template uses `siteBrand` (`Asaastance | …`).
- Product strings in **[`lib/site-content.ts`](lib/site-content.ts)** (agents, pricing, integration, story) use **Asaastance** where the old form appeared.
- **[`components/CTASection.tsx`](components/CTASection.tsx)**, **[`app/what-we-offer/page.tsx`](app/what-we-offer/page.tsx)**, and **[`app/pricing/page.tsx`](app/pricing/page.tsx)** metadata updated similarly.
- Hero display headline stays **`ASAASTANCE`** as in your copy.

### New homepage content (all in `site-content.ts`)
- **`homeHeroVisual`** — hero quote text.
- **`homeTrustedBy`** — label + partner initials/names.
- **`homeSpotlightsSection`** — eyebrow, title, CTA link, three spotlight cards (copy aligned to agents/ops; **not** legacy case-study text).
- **`homeLeadership`** — leadership block intro + CTA.

### New sections
- **[`components/HomeTrustedBySection.tsx`](components/HomeTrustedBySection.tsx)** — proof strip with initial badges.
- **[`components/HomeSpotlightsSection.tsx`](components/HomeSpotlightsSection.tsx)** — three stat-style cards with illustrations from `public/`.
- **[`components/HomeLeadershipSection.tsx`](components/HomeLeadershipSection.tsx)** — real **`aboutContent.founders`** + `/portrait-placeholder.svg` (with proper `alt`).

### Visual lift (A)
- **[`app/globals.css`](app/globals.css)** — **`bg-brand-mesh`**, stronger **`--shadow-elevated`**, **`hero-panel-float`** animation (only when **`prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference`**).
- **[`components/HeroSection.tsx`](components/HeroSection.tsx)** — mesh layers, stronger panel depth, float on the visual, quote from **`homeHeroVisual`**, attribution **`siteBrand.name`**.
- **[`components/HomeWhatWeDoSection.tsx`](components/HomeWhatWeDoSection.tsx)** — mesh wash, stronger hover, gradient accent bar per card, **Explore {siteBrand.name} Agents** link.

### Page order & cleanup
- **[`app/page.tsx`](app/page.tsx)** — `Hero` → `TrustedBy` → `WhatWeDo` → `Spotlights` → `HowItWorks` → `Leadership` → `CTA`.
- **`FoundersTeaser.tsx`** removed (replaced by **`HomeLeadershipSection`**).

### Check
- **`npm run build`** completes successfully.

To swap in real founder photos later, add files under `public/` and extend founder entries in `site-content` with optional `imageSrc` fields, then map them in **`HomeLeadershipSection`**.

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what was implemented to mirror [asaastance.vercel.app](https://asaastance.vercel.app/) and improve type scale and responsiveness.

### Color palette (from production CSS)
Semantic tokens now match the live app: **primary `#0766ee`**, **ink `#020618`**, **slate neutrals** (`#f1f5f9` muted, `#62748e` muted text, `#e2e8f0` borders), **destructive**, **ring**, **sidebar**, and **chart** colors for light and dark. The old sky-blue (`#5DADE2`) primary is replaced so local UI matches production.

### Typography
- In `@theme inline`, **Tailwind text steps** are bumped so `text-sm` ≈ 15px, `text-base` **1.125rem (18px)**, and headings scale up (e.g. `text-4xl`–`text-7xl` slightly larger).
- **`body`** uses `text-base`, `antialiased`, and **`line-height: 1.6`**.
- **`p`** uses **`clamp(1rem, 0.16vw + 0.96rem, 1.125rem)`** (~16–18px) and **`line-height: 1.65`** so long copy stays readable on small widths without blowing up layout.
- **`html`** includes **`-webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%`** so mobile browsers don’t shrink type unexpectedly.

### Other
- **`bg-brand-mesh`** uses **`color-mix`** with `var(--primary)` / `var(--foreground)` so the mesh follows the new blue.
- **`/experts`** drops hardcoded grays for **`bg-card`**, **`border-border`**, **`text-muted-foreground`**, etc., and the grid pattern fill uses **`#0766ee`**.

`npm run build` completes successfully. If you want the wordmark or any remaining `slate-*` utilities elsewhere aligned the same way, say which routes to prioritize.

Here’s what changed.

### New comparison layout (`VsComparisonBoard.tsx`)
- **Two-column board** (stacks on small screens, **Us first**): matches the “elevated vs flat” pattern from your second reference.
- **Gradient side tabs** like the Do’s / Don’ts mock: **emerald** strip + thumbs-up for **Us**, **amber → red** + thumbs-down for **Them**; on mobile the tab is a **horizontal band** with icon + label; from `sm` up the label runs **vertical** on the strip.
- **White / card panel** slightly **overlaps** the tab (`-ml-4`) for depth.
- **Us**: strong **shadow**, ring, **green gradient** check circles, **foreground** copy.
- **Them**: **lighter surface**, minimal shadow, **warm gradient** X circles, **muted** copy.
- Lists use semantic **`<ul>` / `<li>`** with spacing and dividers for scanability.

### Page + copy (`page.tsx`, `site-content.ts`)
- Replaced the table and stacked mobile cards with the new board.

Here’s what changed:

### Us (positive)
- **Tab & row icons** use a **blue ramp** tied to your brand: `sky-300` → **`primary` (`#0766ee`)** → `blue-950`, with dark mode using `blue-400` → `primary` → **`background`** so it lands on the same deep ink as the rest of the site.

### Them (negative)
- **Tab & row icons** use **neutral slate** gradients (`slate-300` → `slate-800` in light, deeper slates in dark) so the contrast is **structure vs noise**, not orange/red, while staying in the same **slate + ink** world as [asaastance.vercel.app](https://asaastance.vercel.app/).

So the comparison still reads as “better vs typical,” but the color story is **primary blue + slate**, not traffic-light green/red. If you want the “them” column to pull even closer to `muted` / `border` tokens only, we can nudge those gradients next.

      <div className="lg:col-span-7 flex flex-col gap-3 border-l-2 border-primary pl-5 ... lg:row-start-1 lg:row-end-4 self-stretch">
        Eyebrow
        h1
        p tagline
        p body  
        buttons
      </div>
```

Wait - if we want bento, we might split into tiles:
- Tile A: eyebrow + h1
- Tile B: image (tall, right)
- Tile C: tagline 
- Tile D: body
- Tile E: buttons

Or simpler bento 2x2 text + 1 tall image:

```
[ Eyebrow + H1           ] [ Image    ]
[ Tagline + body + btns  ] [ panel    ]
```

So left column stacked tight, right column one cell that spans 2 rows visually.

```tsx
<div className="grid gap-3 md:grid-cols-12 md:gap-4 lg:items-stretch">
  <div className="flex flex-col gap-3 border-l-4 border-primary pl-5 md:col-span-7 md:gap-4">
    <Eyebrow />
    <h1 />
    <p className="text-primary font-semibold">{tagline}</p>
    <p className="text-muted-foreground">{body}</p>
    <div className="flex gap-3 ...">buttons</div>
  </div>
  <div className="md:col-span-5 md:row-span-1 min-h-[280px] ...">
    image card
  </div>
</div>
```

CSS grid: one row on md, two columns - left takes 7, right takes 5 but right needs to match height of left. Use `md:row-span-2` - need two rows on left:

Actually:
```tsx
<div className="grid auto-rows-min gap-3 md:grid-cols-12 md:gap-4">
  <div className="md:col-span-7 space-y-3 border-l-4 border-primary pl-5 md:pr-2">
    ...all text and buttons stacked with space-y-3
  </div>
  <div className="relative min-h-[260px] overflow-hidden rounded-2xl md:col-span-5 md:row-start-1 md:row-end-[span_999] self-stretch">
```

`row-span-999` isn't valid - use `md:row-span-2` with grid defining 2 rows:

```tsx
<div className="grid gap-3 md:grid-cols-12 md:grid-rows-[auto_1fr] md:gap-4">
  <div className="md:col-span-7 md:row-span-1 space-y-3 border-l-4 border-primary pl-5">
    eyebrow, h1, tagline
  </div>
  <div className="md:col-span-5 md:row-span-2 md:row-start-1">
    image
  </div>
  <div className="md:col-span-7 md:row-span-1 space-y-3 border-l-4 border-primary pl-5 border-t-0">
    body, buttons
  </div>
</div>
```

But then left has split with duplicate border-l - better one wrapper:

```tsx
<div className="grid gap-3 md:grid-cols-12 md:grid-rows-[auto_1fr]">
  <div className="md:col-span-7 md:row-span-2 flex flex-col gap-3 border-l-4 border-primary pl-5">
    eyebrow, h1, tagline, body, buttons - all tight space-y-3
  </div>
  <div className="md:col-span-5 md:row-span-2 min-h-[300px]">
    card
  </div>
</div>
```

Both spanning row-span-2 = one row of grid? In a 12-col grid with 2 rows, if left is col-span-7 row-span-2 and right is col-span-5 row-span-2, we need only 1 logical row in grid - actually both in same row:

```tsx
<div className="grid gap-3 md:grid-cols-12 md:items-stretch">
  <div className="md:col-span-7 flex flex-col justify-center gap-3 border-l-4 border-primary pl-5 py-1">
    ...
  </div>
  <div className="md:col-span-5">
    <div className="relative h-full min-h-[320px] lg:min-h-[380px]">img card</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

That's the classic 7+5 split without huge gaps - reduce padding.

3. Remove HomeTrustedBySection from page.tsx and remove import

4. HomeWhatWeDoSection - fix bento to no void:
Current: i===0 col-span-8, i===1 col-span-4, i===2 col-span-6 col-start-7
That's row1: 8+4=12, row2: card2 is only in row1... actually card1 row1 col 1-8, card2 row1 col 9-12, card3 row2 col 7-12 (6 cols) leaving col 1-6 empty bottom left = the void.

Fix: Use a proper bento:
Option A: 2x2 where card1 is top-left large (8), card2 top-right (4), card3 bottom-left (6), small accent tile bottom-right (6) - we only have 3 cards so:
- Row1: card1 (8) + card2 (4)
- Row2: card3 (12) full width - or card3 (6) + empty replaced by extending card3

Option B: 
- Row1: card1 (6) + card2 (6) equal
- Row2: card3 (12) full width

Option C: true bento
- card1: col-span-7 row-span-2 (tall left)
- card2: col-span-5 (top right)
- card3: col-span-5 (bottom right) 

That fills: 
```
[ A     ] [ B ]
[ A     ] [ C ]
```
A spans 2 rows, B and C stack on right. 7+5=12, good.

Implement:
```tsx
i===0 && "md:col-span-7 md:row-span-2",
i===1 && "md:col-span-5 md:row-start-1 md:col-start-8",
i===2 && "md:col-span-5 md:row-start-2 md:col-start-8",
```

Grid: `md:grid-cols-12 md:grid-rows-2 md:gap-4 md:auto-rows-fr`

Card A needs min-height on row span 2. Card B and C equal height on the right.

5. Reduce Section padding for What we do - `mb-12` to `mb-6` or integrate header into grid:

```tsx
<div className="grid md:grid-cols-12 gap-4">

```

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what changed:

### Hero (`HeroSection.tsx`)
- **Tighter layout**: Dropped the tall `min-h-[92vh]` block and large `py-16`/`gap-16` spacing. Uses a **12-column bento-style grid** (`gap-3` / `gap-4`): **7 cols** for copy (with the **blue left rule** on the whole text stack), **5 cols** for the visual.
- **Image**: Replaced `next/image` with a native **`<img>`** so the asset reliably **fills** the frame: `absolute inset-0 h-full w-full object-cover object-center`, plus `width`/`height`, `decoding="async"`, and `fetchPriority="high"`. Source: **`/hero-visual.svg`** (the UI-style illustration already in `public`).
- **Copy block**: Single column with consistent `gap-3` / `gap-3.5` so eyebrow, headline, tagline, body, and CTAs read as one tile without extra air.

### Removed subsection
- **`HomeTrustedBySection`** is no longer rendered on the home page (`app/page.tsx`).

### What we do — bento without the hole (`HomeWhatWeDoSection.tsx`)
- **Grid**: `md:grid-rows-2` with  
  - **Card 1**: `md:col-span-7 md:row-span-2` (tall left)  
  - **Card 2**: `md:col-span-5 md:row-start-1 md:col-start-8`  
  - **Card 3**: `md:col-span-5 md:row-start-2 md:col-start-8`  
  So the layout is **one tall tile + two stacked tiles**—no empty bottom-left cell.
- **Tighter rhythm**: Smaller gaps, slightly reduced header margin, slightly tighter section vertical padding override, `md:h-full` on tiles, modest `md:min-h` on the grid so rows stay even.

`HomeTrustedBySection.tsx` is still in the repo if you want it elsewhere later. To use a **PNG/JPEG** hero instead of the SVG, drop the file in `public/` and change the `src` on that `<img>`.

_**User**_

Put it in center.

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user wants to center something - likely the OfferAgentsToc navigation pills section on /what-we-offer that we just made full width. They want the content centered instead of left-aligned.

I'll read OfferAgentsToc.tsx and center the nav content - typically `justify-center` on the flex nav, but with overflow-x-auto the pills might need to be centered when they fit and scroll when they overflow. Common pattern: use `justify-center` when content is smaller than container, but that breaks scroll alignment on mobile when overflow...

Actually for overflow-x-auto with justify-center, when content overflows, centering can be weird. Options:
1. `justify-center flex-wrap` - wraps pills on small screens
2. `md:justify-center` with `justify-start` on mobile where scroll matters
3. Center the inner flex with `mx-auto` on a flex container that's `justify-center` and allow scroll - use `justify-center min-w-max` wrapper inside overflow-x-auto

Pattern for centered scrollable row:
```tsx
<div className="overflow-x-auto">
  <div className="flex justify-center gap-2 min-w-min">
    ...
  </div>
</div>
```

When items overflow, the inner min-w-min grows to content width and user scrolls - the scroll position starts at what browser decides. Often people use `justify-center` on the outer flex with `flex-wrap` for smaller screens.

Simpler interpretation: user wants pills centered in the viewport when there's extra space - `justify-center` on the nav.

```tsx
<nav className="flex w-full min-w-0 justify-center gap-2 overflow-x-auto ...">
```

When overflow happens on small screens, justify-center might clip equally - actually with overflow-x-auto, flex with justify-center centers the flex items as a group - if total width > container, overflow works and you can scroll from center... Actually the start of scroll might not show first items. Let me check.

For flex + overflow-x-auto + justify-center: when flex items total width exceeds container, the default behavior can be problematic. Best approach:
- Use a wrapper: outer `flex justify-center overflow-x-auto w-full`, inner `flex gap-2` with `w-max` or `inline-flex` so the inner is only as wide as content, centered when smaller than viewport.

```tsx
<div className="w-full overflow-x-auto">
  <nav className="mx-auto flex w-max max-w-full justify-center gap-2 ...">
```

Actually `w-max` makes nav as wide as content. Parent overflow-x-auto. To center when smaller: `flex justify-center` on parent:

```tsx
<div className="flex w-full justify-center overflow-x-auto pb-1 ...">
  <nav className="flex gap-2" style={{ width: 'max-content' }}>
```

Or:
```tsx
<div className="w-full overflow-x-auto">
  <nav className="mx-auto flex w-fit max-w-none gap-2 ...">
```

`mx-auto` on nav inside overflow-x-auto - the nav is w-fit (as wide as children), mx-auto centers it when parent is wider.

```tsx
<div className="w-full overflow-x-auto pb-1 ...">
  <nav className="mx-auto flex w-max gap-2 ..." aria-label="...">
```

`w-max` = width: max-content. `mx-auto` centers the block inside the overflow container when the block is narrower than the container. When wider, the block extends and you scroll.

Let me read current OfferAgentsToc file and apply this.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s how your repo lines up with [asaastance.vercel.app](https://asaastance.vercel.app/) and where that **8-card “Comprehensive Digital Solutions”** block fits best.

### How your app differs today
- **Home** uses **`HomeWhatWeDoSection`**: three pillars (AI / SaaS / consultancy) — high level, not the eight line-of-business tiles from production.
- **`/what-we-offer`** is a **long-form “agents”** page (TOC, deep sections). That’s a different job than the marketing **service catalog** on the live site.

So the Vercel block isn’t really “missing” from one place; it’s a **second layer**: named offerings + short blurbs + two tags each.

### Sensible places to put it

1. **Homepage (best match to Vercel)**  
   Add it **after the hero** or **after “What we do”** (and before spotlights / how it works).  
   - Same story as production: hero → trust / value → **full solutions grid** → proof / process / team → CTA.  
   - Keeps **`/what-we-offer`** for people who want depth; the grid can link there or to future `/services/...` URLs.

2. **New `/services` (or `/solutions`) index**  
   If you want the nav to say **“Services”** like many enterprise sites: put the **full grid on that page** and link “What we offer” or “Learn more” from home. Good when the homepage should stay shorter.

3. **Only at the top of `/what-we-offer`**  
   Works if you treat that URL as **“everything we sell”** and the grid is the **overview**, with the current TOC as **detail**. Weaker if visitors expect “agents only” and get a big marketing grid first—then **home + optional `/services`** is clearer.

### Practical recommendation
- **Primary:** add **`Comprehensive Digital Solutions` (8 cards) on the home page** after **`HomeWhatWeDoSection`**, with each card linking to a route you’ll add later (e.g. `/services/blog-automation`) or to **`/what-we-offer`** as a temporary anchor.  
- **Optional:** add **`/services`** later if you outgrow one long homepage.

If you want this implemented next, say whether you prefer **home-only**, **new `/services`**, or **both**, and we can wire content in `site-content` and a small grid component to match the live layout.

  {/* left cell */}
  {/* center: line + dot */}
  {/* right cell */}
</div>
```

For even i: left = pill, right = card
For odd i: left = card, right = pill

Center column: w-px bg-border relative, dot absolute left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2

Pill: gradient from primary to a slightly different blue - use `bg-gradient-to-r from-primary to-sky-500` or `from-primary via-primary to-blue-600` with white text, rounded-full px-4 py-2, shadow-lg shadow-primary/25

Card: bg-card dark:bg-zinc-900 rounded-2xl shadow-md border border-border/80 p-6 - match DigitalSolutionsGrid

Subtle watermark: optional CSS gradient circle or very low opacity pattern - use `absolute inset-0 rounded-2xl opacity-[0.04] bg-[radial-gradient(circle_at_90%_20%,var(--primary),transparent_50%)]` or simple decorative circle

Implementation:

```tsx
function extractPillLabel(title: string): string {
  const m = title.match(/\(([^)]+)\)\s*$/);
  return m ? m[1] : `Step ${i+1}`;
}

function extractHeading(title: string): string {
  return title.replace(/\s*\([^)]+\)\s*$/, "").trim();
}
```

<div className={cn(
  "relative grid gap-8 pb-16 md:grid-cols-[1fr_min-content_1fr] md:gap-8 md:pb-20",
)}>

  <div className={cn(
    "flex md:justify-end",

Better structure:

```tsx
<section className="relative">
  {/* vertical line absolute center hidden on mobile */}
  <div className="absolute left-1/2 top-0 bottom-0 hidden w-px -translate-x-1/2 bg-border md:block" />
  
  {steps.map((step, i) => (
    <div key={} className="relative mb-16 grid md:grid-cols-2 md:gap-12">
      {i % 2 === 0 ? (
        <>
          <div className="md:text-right md:pr-8 flex md:justify-end items-start pt-2">
            <Pill />
          </div>
          <div className="md:pl-8">
            <Card />
          </div>
        </>
      ) : (
        <>
          <div className="md:order-2 md:text-left md:pl-8 flex md:justify-start items-start pt-2">
            <Pill />
          </div>
          <div className="md:order-1 md:pr-8 md:text-right">
            <Card className="md:ml-auto" /> // card on left side visually - max-w
          </div>
        </>
      )}
      {/* dot on center line */}
      <div className="absolute left-1/2 top-8 hidden size-4 -translate-x-1/2 rounded-full border-4 border-background bg-primary md:block" />
    </div>
  ))}
</section>
```

Each row needs `position relative` and the dot at correct vertical - `top-8` might need per-row adjustment. Use `items-start` and put dot aligned with pill vertical center using flex on row.

Actually use grid with 2 cols and pseudo center line on parent:

```tsx
<div className="relative mx-auto max-w-5xl">
  <div className="absolute left-1/2 top-0 bottom-0 hidden w-px -translate-x-1/2 bg-border md:block" aria-hidden />
  {steps.map((step, i) => {
    const isLeftPill = i % 2 === 0;
    return (
      <div key={step.title} className="relative mb-12 md:mb-20">
        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 gap-6 md:grid-cols-2 md:gap-0">
          {isLeftPill ? (
            <>
              <div className="flex justify-center md:justify-end md:pr-12">
                <Pill label={label} />
              </div>
              <div className="md:pl-12">
                <TimelineCard step={step} heading={heading} />
              </div>
            </>
          ) : (
            <>
              <div className="flex justify-center md:order-2 md:justify-start md:pl-12">
                <Pill label={label} />
              </div>
              <div className="md:order-1 md:flex md:justify-end md:pr-12">
                <TimelineCard step={step} heading={heading} className="md:max-w-lg w-full" />
              </div>
            </>
          )}
        </div>
        <div className="absolute left-1/2 top-6 hidden size-3.5 -translate-x-1/2 rounded-full border-[3px] border-background bg-primary shadow-md shadow-primary/40 md:block" />
      </div>
    );

```tsx
"use client";

import { useEffect, useState } from "react";

export function Navbar() {
  const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!isOpen) return;
    const prev = document.body.style.overflow;
    document.body.style.overflow = "hidden";
    return () => {
      document.body.style.overflow = prev;
    };
  }, [isOpen]);

  return (
    <>
      <div
        className={cn(
          "fixed inset-x-0 top-20 bottom-0 z-40 flex flex-col bg-background lg:hidden",
          "transition-[visibility,opacity] duration-200",
          isOpen ? "visible opacity-100" : "invisible opacity-0 pointer-events-none"
        )}
        id="mobile-menu"
        aria-hidden={!isOpen}
      >
        <nav className="flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col overflow-y-auto overscroll-y-contain px-6 pb-8 pt-6" aria-label="Mobile">
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-1 border-b border-border pb-6">
            {navLinks.map((link) => (
              <Link
                key={link.name}
                href={link.href}
                className="rounded-lg px-3 py-3.5 font-display text-lg font-semibold leading-snug text-foreground hover:bg-muted"
                onClick={() => setIsOpen(false)}
              >
                {link.name}
              </Link>
            ))}
          </div>
          <div className="mt-6 shrink-0">
            <Link href="/inquiry" onClick={() => setIsOpen(false)} className="block">
              <Button size="lg" className="w-full rounded-full">
                Get Started
              </Button>
            </Link>
          </div>
        </nav>
      </div>

      <nav className="sticky top-0 z-50 flex h-20 ...">
        ...
      </nav>
    </>
  );
}
```

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
The mobile menu lived **inside** the same `<nav>` that uses **`backdrop-blur`**. That creates a **stacking/filter containing block**, so the menu’s **`position: fixed`** was tied to the nav instead of the viewport. The overlay was **clipped**, **semi-transparent** (`bg-background/98`), and could sit **under** things like sticky sections—so page content and buttons looked like they were **on top of** or **mixed with** the menu.

### Fixes
1. **Drawer outside `<nav>`** — The mobile panel is a **sibling** of the nav (wrapped in a fragment). It’s **`fixed`** with `top: 5rem` (below the bar), **`z-40`**, and a **solid `bg-background`** (no transparency).
2. **Bar stays on top** — Main `<nav>` stays **`sticky z-50`**; logo and menu button use **`z-[60]`** so they stay tappable above the drawer.
3. **Scroll** — Inner area uses **`flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-y-auto overscroll-y-contain`** so long lists scroll instead of overlapping; **`body { overflow: hidden }`** while open so the page doesn’t scroll behind.
4. **Layout** — Links are a **`<ul>`** with **`block` links**, **`py-3.5`**, **`leading-normal`**, and a small **`gap-0.5`** so text isn’t clipped. **Get Started** is **`shrink-0`** below the list so it doesn’t float over links.
5. **Route changes** — Menu **closes on navigation** (`pathname` in a `useEffect`).
6. **A11y** — **`aria-controls` / `id`**, **`aria-expanded`**, **`aria-hidden`** on the drawer.

The **“AGENTS”** block in your screenshot isn’t in this `Navbar`; if it still appears after this fix, it’s likely another sticky layer—we can track that down next if needed.

  <ul className="mt-5 grid grid-cols-1 gap-2 sm:grid-cols-2 ... mx-auto max-w-xs sm:max-w-none sm:mx-0 justify-items-start ... md:justify-items-start">
```

For mobile center: `justify-items-center` on grid for single column centers each li's content... Actually grid items are full cell width. `text-center` on ul centers text in each cell. For agents list with long titles, `text-center` works.

```tsx
<ul className="mt-5 space-y-2 text-center sm:grid sm:grid-cols-2 sm:gap-2 sm:text-left md:text-left ...">
```

On mobile flex col:
```tsx
<ul className="mt-5 flex flex-col items-center gap-2 text-sm ... sm:grid sm:grid-cols-2 sm:items-stretch sm:justify-items-start">
```

When sm:grid, items stretch full width of grid cells - text left in cells. When flex col items-center, li are only as wide as content.

I'll use:

```tsx
<div className="lg:col-span-3 flex flex-col items-center md:items-start">
  <h4 className="text-center md:text-left">Explore</h4>
  <ul className="mt-5 flex flex-col items-center gap-3 md:items-start">
```

  <ul className="mt-5 flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col items-center gap-2 sm:max-w-none sm:grid sm:grid-cols-2 sm:items-stretch md:items-stretch ...">
```

On sm grid, `justify-items-center` for mobile-only... Actually below sm it's flex centered. From sm up grid with `sm:justify-items-start` or default stretch with text-left.

```tsx
<ul className="mt-5 flex flex-col items-center gap-2 text-sm text-background/80 sm:grid sm:w-full sm:grid-cols-2 sm:items-start sm:justify-items-start">

Here’s the **deployment readiness pass** and what was fixed.

### ESLint (was failing → **clean**)
1. **`app/case-studies/page.tsx`** — `react/no-unescaped-entities`: fixed `RetailCorp's`, `Let's`, and the testimonial quotes/apostrophe using `&apos;`, `&ldquo;`, `&rdquo;`, `&rsquo;`.
2. **`app/success-stories/page.tsx`** — same rule: `Asaastance's` → `Asaastance&apos;s`.
3. **`components/Navbar.tsx`** — `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`: kept the “close menu on pathname change” behavior and documented it with **`eslint-disable-next-line`** for that line only.
4. **`components/HeroSection.tsx`** — `@next/next/no-img-element`: **`eslint-disable-next-line`** with a short reason (SVG + `object-cover`).

### Critical config bug (**would have broken production**)
In **`next.config.ts`**, **`/services/:path*`** was redirecting **every** service URL to **`/what-we-offer`**, which overrode your real routes like `/services/blog-automation`.

- **Removed** the `/services/:path*` redirect.
- **`/services`** (exact) now redirects to **`/other-services`** (the solutions grid index).

`npm run build` still succeeds and `/services/[slug]` routes are generated.

### Verification
- **`npm run lint`** — exit 0  
- **`npm run build`** — exit 0 (TypeScript included in Next build)

### Vercel note (from your logs)
You still have a **multiple lockfiles** warning (`bun.lock` vs `package-lock.json`). On Vercel, either:

- Deploy from the repo directory that only contains **`package-lock.json`**, or  
- Remove the extra root **`bun.lock`** if you’re not using Bun for this app, or  
- Set **`turbopack.root`** in `next.config` as the message suggests  

so the builder doesn’t pick the wrong root.

---

You’re in good shape to deploy: **lint + build are green**, and **service deep links work** with the updated redirects.

Done. `/inquiry` now sends inquiries to **both**:

- `daniel@asaastance.com`
- `john@asaastance.com`

### What changed
In `app/inquiry/page.tsx`, form submit now:
- collects all form fields,
- builds a prefilled email subject/body,
- opens a `mailto:` addressed to both recipients,
- then routes to `/inquiry/thank-you`.

### Important note
This uses the user’s email client (`mailto:`), so it’s reliable for immediate dual-recipient delivery from the browser context.  
If you want guaranteed backend delivery (no dependency on user mail app), next step is adding a server endpoint (Resend/SMTP) and posting the form there.

---

_**User**_

Does supabase offer this kind of service? I'm looking at https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/auth-smtp 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Short answer: **partly yes**.

Supabase supports SMTP in the way shown in that doc, but it is specifically for **Supabase Auth emails** (signup confirmation, magic links, reset password, invites, etc.), not a generic “contact form relay” by itself.

- With Supabase’s **default SMTP**, sending is limited (team-authorized addresses only, rate limits, best-effort delivery).
- With **custom SMTP configured in Supabase Auth**, it can send to all addresses and is suitable for production auth email flows.
- For your `/inquiry` use case (send inquiry payload to `daniel@...` and `john@...`), you’d typically use:
  - a Supabase **Edge Function / backend endpoint** + email provider API/SMTP, or
  - your current `mailto:` approach (client-dependent), if you accept that limitation.

Reference: [Supabase Auth custom SMTP docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/auth-smtp).

If you want, I can outline the cleanest Supabase architecture for your inquiry form (Edge Function + provider + env vars + spam protection) step by step.

  "plan": "# Supabase Inquiry-Only Lead Pipeline\n\n## Goal\nGet &#96;/inquiry&#96; to reliably capture lead submissions into Supabase with production-safe defaults, so inquiry data is retained even if email delivery fails.\n\n## Scope (Now)\n- Only touch inquiry capture flow.\n- Do not redesign the rest of the app.\n- Prioritize reliability + low maintenance + zero-cost-compatible setup.\n\n## Architecture\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TD\n  user[WebsiteVisitor] --\u003e inquiryForm[/inquiry form]\n  inquiryForm --\u003e apiRoute[Next API Route submitInquiry]\n  apiRoute --\u003e validate[Schema validation + honeypot + rate limit check]\n  validate --\u003e supabaseInsert[Insert into leads.inquiries]\n  supabaseInsert --\u003e outboxInsert[Insert into leads.outbox]\n  outboxInsert --\u003e response[Return success to UI]\n  outboxInsert --\u003e worker[Scheduled worker / Edge Function]\n  worker --\u003e emailSend[Send notification email to Daniel + John]\n  emailSend --\u003e deliveryLog[Update outbox status + attempts]\n  supabaseInsert --\u003e dashboard[Supabase table/view for lead management]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Implementation Plan\n\n### 1) Data model and ownership boundaries\n- Create schema &#96;leads&#96;
